question: The <div> element is an inline-level element by default.
Which CSS property changes text color?
Which of these is NOT part of the CSS Box Model?
CSS Grid and Flexbox cannot be used together in the same layout.
What is the correct CSS specificity order from highest to lowest?
em units in CSS are always relative to the root element's font size.
Which HTML attribute provides alternative text for images?
What does position: sticky do?
options: Fixes the element to the viewport at all times
Keeps an element visible within its parent's bounds as the user scrolls
Removes the element from the document flow
Makes the element float alongside text

question: What does display: contents do?
options: Removes the element's box while keeping its children in the flow
Creates a new block formatting context
Hides the element and all its children

question: What is the CSS contain property used for?
options: Preventing content from overflowing its container
Isolating a subtree from the rest of the document to improve rendering performance

question: Which HTML element is best suited for introductory content at the top of a page or section?
The HTML ul element creates an unordered list.
Which CSS property controls the space between an element's content and its border?
Which HTML element is used to create a hyperlink?
The span element is inline by default.
Which selector targets an element with the class name card?
Which HTML element inserts a line break?
An id value should be unique within an HTML document.
Which CSS property is commonly used to make text bold?
Which CSS pseudo-class applies styles when the pointer is over an element?
In CSS, rem units are relative to which font size?
An absolutely positioned element is positioned relative to the nearest ancestor with a non-static position.
Which HTML input type is used to mask entered characters?
Which CSS property must typically be set for a ::before pseudo-element to render generated content?
The aria-label attribute can provide an accessible name for an element even when there is no visible text label.
Which value of object-fit scales an image to fill its box while preserving aspect ratio and possibly cropping it?
Which meta tag is essential for responsive layouts on mobile devices?
Which selector matches checked checkbox inputs?
What is a key characteristic of display: inline-block?
options: The element always starts on a new line
The element can sit inline while still accepting width and height
The element ignores width and height
The element becomes a flex container

question: A label element can be associated with an input by matching the label's for attribute to the input's id.
Which selector has the highest specificity?
In CSS Grid, what does minmax(200px, 1fr) mean for a track?
options: The track is at least 200px wide and can grow to take a fractional share of free space
The track can shrink to 0 and grow to 200px

question: Setting visibility: hidden hides an element but still preserves its layout space.
Which selector targets anchor elements whose target attribute is set to _blank?
What is the purpose of the CSS aspect-ratio property?
options: It defines a preferred width-to-height ratio for an element's box
It sets the monitor aspect ratio for media queries
It scales font sizes proportionally to viewport height
It crops overflowing content to a given ratio

question: What is the effect of box-sizing: border-box?
options: Padding and border are added outside the declared width and height
The element becomes a border-only box
Only border counts toward the declared width and height
Width and height include the content, padding, and border

question: If two CSS rules have the same specificity and importance, which one wins?
options: The one that appears first in the stylesheet
The one that appears later in the cascade order
Neither; the browser merges them unpredictably

question: A script element with the defer attribute downloads during HTML parsing and executes after parsing is complete.
Which pseudo-element targets the first formatted line of a block of text?
What does font-size: clamp(1rem, 2vw, 2rem) do?
options: It fixes the font size at 2vw
It sets the font size to the smallest of the three values only
It keeps the font size between 1rem and 2rem while preferring 2vw
It alternates between 1rem and 2rem based on breakpoints

question: What is special about the specificity of selectors inside the CSS :where() pseudo-class?
options: They are ignored unless paired with :is()
They only work in media queries

question: In CSS Grid, subgrid allows a nested grid to inherit track sizing from its parent grid.
When an img element uses srcset with x-descriptors like 1x and 2x, what is the browser primarily choosing for?
options: The number of images on the page

question: What does @supports (display: grid) check?
options: Whether the browser supports CSS Grid for the current document
Whether grid items are currently visible
Whether the display property is being inherited
Whether the element has grid-template-columns defined

question: Which selector allows styling a parent element when it contains a matching descendant?
Setting pointer-events: none on an element prevents that element from being the target of pointer events.
What is the main purpose of content-visibility: auto?
options: To let the browser skip rendering work for off-screen content until needed
To create a new stacking context for media elements
To force hidden elements to reserve no layout space

question: What is true about the HTML template element?
options: It can only contain plain text
Its contents are parsed but not rendered until instantiated or cloned
It automatically becomes a shadow DOM root
Its contents render immediately but stay non-interactive

question: What is rel="preload" primarily used for?
options: Delaying low-priority assets until after page load
Combining multiple stylesheets into one request
Hinting that a critical resource should be fetched early
Preventing a resource from being cached

question: Using width: 100vw can sometimes cause horizontal overflow because the viewport width may include the vertical scrollbar.
